heading: Establishment of the Civil Rights in Education: Brown v. Board of Education National Historical Park
label: 410iiii–2 Establishment of the Civil Rights in Education: Brown v. Board of Education National Historical Park

children.heading: In general
children.label: a In general
children.text: There is hereby established as a unit of the National Park System the Brown v. Board of Education National Historical Park in the State of Kansas.

children.heading: Description
children.label: b Description
children.text: The historical park shall consist of the Monroe Elementary School site in the city of Topeka, Shawnee County, Kansas, as generally depicted on a map entitled “Brown v. Board of Education National Historic Site,” numbered Appendix A and dated June 1992. Such map shall be on file and available for public inspection in the appropriate offices of the National Park Service.

children.label: A
children.text: the Summerton High School site in Summerton, Clarendon County, South Carolina;

children.label: B
children.text: the former Scott’s Branch High School site in Summerton, Clarendon County, South Carolina; and

children.label: C
children.text: approximately 1 acre of land adjacent to Monroe Elementary School in Topeka, Shawnee County, Kansas.

children.heading: Map
children.label: 2 Map
children.text: The map described in paragraph (1) shall be on file and available for public inspection in the appropriate offices of the National Park Service.
